lede: A look at May 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Rabindranath Tagore and Johannes Brahms, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2023 Tanur boat disaster
events.desc: Tanur boat disaster: At least 22 people are killed when a boat carrying tourists capsizes in Tanur, Malappuram, Kerala, India.

events.title: Kosovo War
events.desc: Kosovo War: Three Chinese citizens are killed and 20 wounded when a NATO aircraft inadvertently bombs the Chinese embassy in Belgrade, Serbia.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II travels to Romania, becoming the first pope to visit a predominantly Eastern Orthodox country since the Great Schism in 1054.

events.title: Bright Sparklers fireworks disaster
events.desc: A fire and explosion occurs at a fireworks factory at Sungai Buloh, Malaysia, killing 26.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Norway Debate in the British House of Commons begins, and leads to the replacement of Prime Minister Neville Chamberlain with Winston Churchill three days later.

events.title: 1930 Salmas earthquake
events.desc: The 7.1 Mw Salmas earthquake shakes northwestern Iran and southeastern Turkey with a maximum Mercalli intensity of IX (Violent). Up to three-thousand people were killed.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: German submarine U-20 sinks RMS Lusitania, killing 1,199 people, including 128 Americans. Public reaction to the sinking turns many former pro-Germans in the United States against the German Empire.

events.title: Saint Petersburg
events.desc: In Saint Petersburg, Russian scientist Alexander Stepanovich Popov demonstrates to the Russian Physical and Chemical Society his invention, the Popov lightning detector—a primitive radio receiver. In some parts of the former Soviet Union the anniversary of this day is celebrated as Radio Day.

events.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
events.desc: World premiere of Ludwig van Beethoven's Ninth Symphony in Vienna, Austria. The performance is conducted by Michael Umlauf under the composer's supervision.

events.title: French Revolution
events.desc: French Revolution: Robespierre introduces the Cult of the Supreme Being in the National Convention as the new state religion of the French First Republic.

births.name: Rabindranath Tagore
births.line: Indian author and poet, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Josip Broz Tito
births.line: Yugoslav field marshal and politician, 1st President of Yugoslavia

births.name: Eva Perón
births.line: Argentinian actress, 25th First Lady of Argentina

deaths.text: Willard Boyle, Canadian physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Johann Jakob Froberger, German organist and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Harriet Starr Cannon (Episcopal Church (USA))
